# eFounders Design System

> The shared, brand-agnostic design-system base. Projects consume it via shadcn; the
> code is the source of truth. This file tells an AI agent where everything is.

## Start here
- How to use it: Notion — AI-Design Workspace https://app.notion.com/p/3821cc0bd5bc818986acd792a4490721 (setup, onboarding, daily use)
- Conventions template: templates/CLAUDE.project.md (drop into a project as CLAUDE.md so the agent builds on-brand)

## Tokens & components
- Tokens (the contract): app/tokens.css — semantic shadcn tokens + in-house extensions,
  two tiers (primitives → semantic), light + dark. (app/globals.css wires them into Tailwind.)
- Visual identity (portable): DESIGN.md. Agent rules for this repo: CLAUDE.md.
- Component specs (agent-readable): docs/COMPONENTS.md — token intent + component rules.
- Composition (how to combine into screens): docs/COMPOSITION.md — layout primitives (Container/Section/Stack/PageHeader) + page archetypes.
- Component code: components/ui/ — the full shadcn toolbox, themed by the tokens. registry/new-york/ holds the distributed theme.
- Storybook (live reference): https://design-system-template-storybook.vercel.app

## Registry (machine-readable)
- Catalog: registry.json. Built items: public/r/*.json.
- Each built item carries a `meta` contract: status, props, variants, composition rules, related.
- Consume: `shadcn add @efounders/<name>` — registry URL https://design-system-template-registry.vercel.app/r/{name}.json
- Query from an agent: the shadcn MCP (`shadcn mcp init --client claude`).

## Checks
- DS conformance gate: scripts/ds-check.mjs (`npm run ds-check`) + .github/workflows/ds-check.yml —
  hardcoded colors / unknown tokens fail the PR.
- Audit any repo's AI-readiness: the `/ds-audit` skill.
